steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is D, $9mn$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Combine like terms
steps.content: $18mn$ and $9mn$ have the same variable factor, $mn$, so subtract only the coefficients: $$18mn-9mn=(18-9)mn=9mn.$$

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: The equivalent expression is $9mn$, which matches the correct answer.

steps.title: Keep the variable factor and coefficient
steps.content: Because both terms include the same factor $mn$, the result must still include $mn$ with the coefficient from $18-9=9$. Choices A and B drop the variables, and choice C keeps $mn$ but is missing the coefficient 9.
